data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-22T15:12 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.content:
? skogtown-wisp-wisp-037x [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-22 · Updated: 2026-02-22

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-0z5n: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-krhd: Check for orphaned processes ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-nkg0: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-zt1g: Scan agent health ● P2

All children of the patrol wisp are marked as completed (✓). This means the patrol cycle is done. I need to:

1. First update heartbeat
2. Since all steps are complete, squash the wisp and decide whether to loop or exit

Let me first update the heartbeat and check the current mol step.

content.thinking: Multiple completed patrol wisps are stacking up. I need to squash them and start a fresh patrol cycle. Let me check the state file to see patrol count and decide whether to loop or hand off.

content.text: Multiple completed wisps stacked. Let me check state and squash them.

content.thinking:
Patrol count is 15, no extraordinary action. Below the 20 threshold, so I should loop. But first, let me squash the completed wisps and create a fresh patrol.

Let me squash the stacked wisps and then create a new patrol.

content.text: Patrol count 15/20, no extraordinary action. Squashing stale wisps and starting fresh patrol.

input.command: gt mol squash --jitter 10s --summary "Patrol complete: all steps done, cycling to fresh wisp"
input.description: Squash completed patrol wisps to digests
